channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SZA to join Kendrick Lamar for Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Apple Music released a teaser trailer on Thursday showing the Compton native on the gridiron, assessing the field before the big game while he's talking to someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will weigh approval for US’ 1st publicly funded religious charter school, in Oklahoma
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to take on a new culture war dispute: whether the nation’s first publicly funded religious charter school should be allowed to open in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USDA awards $537 million to Colorado power companies
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — The Department of Agriculture (USDA) has announced the award of $537 million to three electric cooperatives in Colorado through the USDA's Empowering Rural America or New ERA program.
item.dc:creator: Alex Rivero, KREX 5

item.title: Trump cites voter ID, water flow as conditions for LA wildfire aid
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he wanted to see two actions taken in California before he offered federal support for Los Angeles as it grapples with wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: London court clears way to extradite US mother accused of killing 2 of her children in Colorado
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A London judge on Friday rejected a U.S. mother's challenge to her extradition to face murder charges in Colorado in the deaths of two of her young children.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What JFK assassination files are still classified? Trump’s order could bring them to light
item.description: Millions of documents related to the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy in Dallas have already been made public, but President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of still-classified files.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests. It didn't fare well.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Just days into the new Trump administration, worries spike amid a show of force on immigration
item.description: Less than a week into his presidency, the Trump administration is touting deportation efforts and has published new rules making it easier to remove people.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini And Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Illegal immigrant admitted to killing woman on her 21st birthday
item.description: Ecuadorian native Jhon Moises Chacaguasay-Ilbis pleaded guilty to murdering 21-year-old Jocelyn Jhoana Toaquiza at a Syracuse, New York, Airbnb on her 21st birthday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: JD Vance speaks at March for Life rally in Washington
item.description: A rally is scheduled for noon on the National Mall and we expect a string of Republicans to speak about their anti-abortion plans.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about President Donald Trump's order targeting the rights of transgender people
item.description: An executive order President Donald Trump signed on his first day back in office offers a new federal government definition of the sexes that could have a major impact on transgender people nationwide.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Purdue Pharma and owners to pay $7.4 billion in settlement of lawsuits over the toll of OxyContin
item.description: Members of the family who own OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma, and the company itself, agreed to pay up to $7.4 billion in a new settlement to lawsuits over the toll of the powerful prescription painkiller, the attorneys general from several states announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Trump order ending federal DEI programs leaves agencies and stakeholders on uncertain ground
item.description: From federal agencies to stakeholders who get federal dollars for special training, many are trying to process how President Donald Trump’s sweeping executive order putting a stop to diversity, equity and inclusion programs across the U.S. government will upend their work.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, AYANNA ALEXANDER and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Post Malone teams up with Oreo on limited-edition cookie flavor
item.description: Oreo has partnered with Post Malone for its newest cookie, featuring a first-of-its-kind filling for the brand.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Wall of ice the size of Rhode Island heading toward penguin-packed island off Antarctica
item.description: The world's biggest iceberg — a wall of ice the size of Rhode Island — is lumbering toward a remote island off Antarctica that's home to millions of penguins and seals.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: CNN to lay off dozens as part of major restructuring
item.description: Cable giant CNN is planning to lay off dozens of employees and undergo a major restructuring as the outlet looks to retool itself around digital during President Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, ABC 4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Vornado space heaters recalled over potential 'shock and fire' hazards
item.description: Thousands of Vornado space heaters sold on Amazon have been recalled amid concerns over possible "electric shock and fire hazards," according to the company.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has moved to end affirmative action in federal contracting and directed that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: President Trump reacts to letter left for him by Biden
item.description: As part of an Inauguration Day tradition, the letter was left inside the Resolute Desk at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump demands apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump early Wednesday morning slammed the reverend at a National Cathedral prayer service for the inauguration who called on him to have mercy on transgender children and immigrant families.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4
